risk_if_not_implemented: Without Standardized Terminology, security events may go undetected, allowing threats to persist unnoticed and increasing breach dwell time.

compensating_control_1.name: Network Segmentation (macrosegementation)
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to ensure network architecture utilizes network segmentation to isolate Technology Assets, Applications and/or Services (TAAS) to protect from other network resources.
compensating_control_1.justification: Network Segmentation (macrosegementation) (NET-06) provides network-level access restriction that compensates for the absence of Standardized Terminology (SEA-02.1) by limiting attacker reach and lateral movement opportunities across the environment. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Alignment With Enterprise Architecture
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to develop an enterprise architecture, aligned with industry-recognized leading practices, with consideration for security, compliance and resilience principles that addresses risk to organizational operations, assets, individuals and other organizations.
compensating_control_2.justification: Alignment With Enterprise Architecture (SEA-02) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of Standardized Terminology (SEA-02.1) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Process applicability. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
